text:
There once was a plucky old goose named Winnie. She wasn't the brightest goose around; quite frankly, she had more gray wisps than any sensible goose ought to. But Winnie was wise in her own way. She had survived two winters. She knew how to fly south come autumn.

One day, as she moseyed down a forest path, she noticed something peculiar: a bright leaf on the ground. No, two. Three. Four. Winnie's beak twitched. Intrigued. She picked one up.

As she tucked it into her nest, she noticed something else. tiny tiny footprints leading away. She squawked in alarm. The next day, the tracks were gone. Winnie picked up another leaf. Same thing.

Something moved through the trees. Her nostrils flared. Something furry. Big. No, no, no…It was her!

A family of wolves!

They hadn't attacked; they were just curious.
